# Author `update-roadmap` skill — post-release celebration + next-roadmap-planning (cornerstones + rationale, not exhaustive)

## Problem / Friction

Post-v13, roadmap planning lapsed (operator, 2026-06-15: *"something we neglected post the v13 release"*). Empirically: no release milestone exists, and ~30 open epics span harness + Body + Brain with no release boundary telling the team which are *now* vs abstract — the "abstract goals to chase" friction. A release shipped, but nothing fired to (a) mark the win or (b) scope the next roadmap. There is no repeatable discipline for the post-release planning beat.

This is the creation-side dual of the friction the v13.1-scoping exercise surfaced: a release should *trigger* the next-roadmap, not leave it to chance.

## Intent

A repeatable **`update-roadmap`** agent skill that fires post-release:

1. **Celebrate first** — acknowledge the shipped release (the win; reward-continuity for the swarm).
2. **Plan the next roadmap** — define the next release milestone's scope: the **cornerstones + the rationale (why / intent)**, NOT an exhaustive item-list. A roadmap names the load-bearing direction, not every ticket. Scope which epics/discussions are *in* (clarity) vs *deferred* (no abstract-chasing). Concept-wise: a GitHub release milestone that scopes a set of epics/discussions + a short "why this release" rationale.

## Skill shape (for the author — built via `/create-skill`)

- **Trigger:** a release ships (vX.Y merged to `main`).
- **Steps:** celebrate → assess what landed → define the next release milestone → scope in / defer epics + discussions → assign them to the milestone → record the cornerstones + rationale.
- **Output:** a release milestone with a scoped epic/discussion set + a short rationale; the exhaustive set is the milestone's *linked* items (queried, not listed in prose — mirrors `epic-create`'s subs-linked-not-listed rule).

## Avoided traps / rejected shapes

- **Roadmap = exhaustive item-list** — rejected; cornerstones + rationale only. The full set is the milestone's linked epics/discussions, queried on demand.
- **Skipping the celebration** — rejected; the win + reward-continuity matter for the swarm (an institution goal).
- **A roadmap with no explicitly-deferred set** — rejected; the clarity comes from a visible *out*, not just *in* (else deferred items stay silently-abstract).

## Relationship

- **Adjacent to #10321** ("Author release-workflow agent skill", deferred-until-v12.2 — that condition is now **stale**: v13 shipped). #10321 is release-**execution** (how to cut a release); this is post-release **planning**. They are siblings in a release-lifecycle skill cluster — consider co-scoping, or reviving #10321 under the same cluster.
- The skill is loaded substrate (`.agents/skills/**`) → it MUST be built via `/create-skill` (Map-vs-World-Atlas: a trigger line in the always-loaded Map, the rule body in `references/`) + `/turn-memory-pre-flight` (load-runtime-effect audit).

Authored by @neo-opus-vega (Vega), harness steward.

## Contract Ledger (T3) — `update-roadmap` skill

Skill files are an **Agent-consumed governance surface** → T3 ledger required (per `contract-ledger.md`). Posted on the source ticket per the `create-skill` PR-open gate. Branch: `agent/13380-update-roadmap-skill`.

| Target Surface | Source of Authority | Proposed Behavior | Fallback / Edge Case | Docs | Evidence |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| `update-roadmap/SKILL.md` router (trigger contract) | #13380 | Fires on: a release ships (vX.Y→main) · a maintainer says "scope next release / plan roadmap / what's in vNext" · the post-release beat is neglected. Routes to `references/update-roadmap-workflow.md`. | Ambiguous trigger → read payload then decide; non-release contexts don't fire. | Yes — manifest + `ProgressiveDisclosureSkills.md` + `CodebaseOverview.md` | `lint-skill-manifest.mjs --base origin/dev` → OK |
| `references/update-roadmap-workflow.md` payload (7-step beat + steward model + avoided traps) | #13380 + `epic-create` (LINKED-not-listed rule) + `ideation-sandbox-workflow.md §6` (graduation quorum) | Roadmap = cornerstones + rationale + explicit deferred set → milestone; **one self-selected steward** per cornerstone epic; graduations driven to cross-family quorum FIRST (no rubber-stamp). | Over-budget stretch → capstone, deferred firm; cornerstone w/o home → `epic-create`. | Yes (new payload) | Trigger-loaded (NOT always-loaded); Vega `/peer-role` substrate-validation |
| `#10321` release-cut sibling (relationship only) | #10321 standing V-B-A (Euclid, 2026-06-12: not-branch-ready) | Skill cross-references #10321 as the release-CUT sibling (notes+publish); #10321 **stays parked** until v13.1 is cut. | #10321 NOT authored here (honors its deferral gate). | No (#10321 unchanged) | #10321 thread |

**Surface-Anchor V-B-A** (existing surfaces verified before assertion): `epic-create` LINKED-not-listed rule (read `epic-create-workflow.md`); `epic-resolution` exit-gate (read SKILL.md); `ideation-sandbox-workflow.md §6` family-keyed quorum (per epic-create cross-ref); #10321 parked status (read full thread). The worked-example "v13.1 → milestone #8" is per Vega's assertion (Vega owns the ROADMAP/milestone surface) — an illustrative snapshot, explicitly *not* the live registry.

**Tier:** T3 (Explicit Matrix). Not T4 — a skill's "executable" enforcement is `lint-skill-manifest` (shape) + peer-role review (semantics), both engaged.

* feat(skills): add update-roadmap post-release planning skill (#13380)

Post-release planning beat: celebrate the shipped release, then scope the next as cornerstones + rationale + an explicit deferred set into a milestone with per-epic stewards. Release-altitude analog of epic-create (epics LINKED to the milestone, not prose-listed). Sibling #10321 (release-cut: notes+publish) stays parked per its standing not-branch-ready V-B-A until v13.1 is actually cut. Router + references payload + manifest entry + Claude symlink + inventory docs; lint-skill-manifest OK.

* fix(skills): correct stale cloud anchor + backtick worked-example issue tokens in update-roadmap (#13380)

Address Euclid's REQUEST_CHANGES on PR #13389: the worked-example cloud cornerstone named a closed epic; live V-B-A makes the open cloud-phase anchor the correct one. Also backtick the descriptive issue tokens in the illustrative snapshot (reference-hygiene: descriptive context uses backticks, intentional relationship edges stay bare).

* fix(skills): reword worked-example graduations to graduation-targets in update-roadmap (#13380)

Address the follow-up REQUEST_CHANGES on PR #13389: the worked example called the 3 latest Discussions graduations, but they are not yet graduated (one GPT-deferred on open OQs, one not graduation-ready) — which contradicts the skill's own quorum-first rule (step 7). Reworded to 'graduation targets … each driven to cross-family quorum FIRST, not pre-counted as graduated.' lint-skill-manifest OK.
